members.name: swarm-s1
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Review current product

**Task ID:** s1
**Title:** Review current product

Perform a comprehensive review of the current product codebase and user-facing behavior. Actions and deliverables:
- Inspect the repository in the current working directory (and any obvious sibling folders) to locate the product source, README, docs, config, CLI or UI code.
- Produce a concise technical summary (architecture, main components, data flow), a features list (what is implemented and how it works), and usage notes (CLI commands, UI flows, limitations, known bugs or UX pain points).
- Identify gaps and missing features compared to a modern AI-assisted coding CLI/service (e.g., authentication, session management, language support, extensions, plugin system, codegen/sourcemap handling, offline support, telemetry/analytics, error handling, tests, CI/CD workflows).
- For each gap or limitation, add a short rationale and a rough implementation complexity estimate (Small/Medium/Large) and a priority suggestion (High/Medium/Low).
- Save outputs as Markdown files in target/temp/swarmout: product_review.md (detailed review), product_features_list.md (concise bullet list), and product_gaps.csv (CSV with columns: gap_id,description,priority,complexity,notes).
Notes: write clearly so other agents can consume these outputs. If parts of the codebase or docs are missing, document what could not be found and why.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s1" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

members.name: swarm-s2
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Analyze competitor: ClaudeCode (../claude-code-sourcemap)

**Task ID:** s2
**Title:** Analyze competitor: ClaudeCode (../claude-code-sourcemap)

Perform a focused competitor analysis of ClaudeCode using the local source at ../claude-code-sourcemap. Actions and deliverables:
- Read the repository at ../claude-code-sourcemap thoroughly (README, source files, docs, tests). Identify core features, supported workflows, strengths, weaknesses, unique capabilities (e.g., sourcemap handling, language support, integrations).
- Summarize architecture, key modules, deployment options, UX (CLI/UI), and any integration points (APIs, plugins).
- Extract a list of features that our product currently lacks but ClaudeCode provides, and mark which are high-impact and feasible to implement.
- Provide recommended features inspired by ClaudeCode, including implementation notes, estimated complexity (Small/Medium/Large), and priority.
- Save outputs as Markdown: analysis_claudecode.md and suggested_features_claudecode.csv (columns: feature_id,feature,benefit,complexity,priority,implementation_notes).
- If the local path is missing or incomplete, document what could not be read and fallback to reading any available docs in repo; report uncertainties.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s2" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

## Swarm Task: Analyze competitor: copilot-cli

**Task ID:** s3
**Title:** Analyze competitor: copilot-cli

Perform a competitor analysis for copilot-cli. Actions and deliverables:
- Attempt to find a local repository at ../copilot-cli. If present, analyze README, source, and docs. If not present, try to locate any docs or manifests in sibling folders; if no local source exists, perform an analysis based on available local docs and best-effort public knowledge (explicitly mark assumptions and uncertainties).
- Identify copilot-cli's core features, UX patterns, integrations (e.g., editor/IDE integration, chat, prompt templates, auth), pricing/operational model (if known), and technical approach.
- List actionable features that would be valuable to implement in our product inspired by copilot-cli, with implementation notes and complexity/priority estimates.
- Save outputs as Markdown: analysis_copilot-cli.md and suggested_features_copilot-cli.csv (columns: feature_id,feature,benefit,complexity,priority,implementation_notes).
- Clearly annotate any assumptions made due to missing local source material.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s3" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

## Swarm Task: Analyze competitor: OpenCode (../opencode)

**Task ID:** s4

Perform a competitor analysis of OpenCode using the local source at ../opencode. Actions and deliverables:
- Read the repository at ../opencode (README, source, docs, examples). Identify primary features, architecture, extensibility points, and notable implementation patterns.
- Highlight any unique functionality or UX that differentiates OpenCode (e.g., plugin architecture, language model integrations, debugging or code navigation features).
- Produce a list of features OpenCode has that our product lacks, with rationale, estimated complexity, and priority.

- Save outputs as: analysis_opencode.md and suggested_features_opencode.csv (feature_id,feature,benefit,complexity,priority,implementation_notes).

members.name: swarm-s6
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Create SWARMOUT2.md: consolidated recommendations and implementation plan

**Task ID:** s6
**Title:** Create SWARMOUT2.md: consolidated recommendations and implementation plan

Consolidate the product review and all four competitor analyses into a single strategic document SWARMOUT2.md. This task depends on s1, s2, s3, s4, s5. Actions and deliverables:
- Read the outputs produced by s1, s2, s3, s4, s5 (product_review.md, analysis_*.md, and their suggested_features_*.csv files) located in target/temp/swarmout.
- Synthesize a prioritized list of feature additions (aggregated, de-duplicated) with a short justification for each (market/technical/user impact) and an estimated complexity and priority.
- Produce a multi-phase implementation plan with Milestones (e.g., MVP, v1, v2), and under each milestone list concrete tasks, owners (roles rather than people), acceptance criteria, estimated effort per task (in story points or person-days), dependencies between tasks, and suggested timelines.
- Include risk assessment, key technical decisions, and a migration/compatibility plan if new features impact existing workflows.
- Produce a high-level Gantt-style milestone schedule (textual) and a CSV tasks_plan.csv with columns: milestone,task_id,task_description,owner_role,est_effort,dependencies,acceptance_criteria.
- Save the final consolidated Markdown as target/temp/swarmout/SWARMOUT2.md along with tasks_plan.csv. Ensure the top of SWARMOUT2.md includes a short executive summary and a table of contents.
Notes: if any required inputs from s1-s5 are missing at runtime, clearly mark which analyses were incomplete and proceed with best-effort consolidation, annotating uncertainties.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s6" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.
